When Hatteras leaves the [[the-forward|Forward]] to press north, the mutineers seize their chance. Shandon made no attempt to restrain them; it was his policy to make them believe that their privations and toils were at an end (ACH-034-017), and the abandoned brig was given to the flames.

To reach the Grand Duke, he takes a captured despatch and presents himself under a false identity — "the only name they could give to Ivan Ogareff" was Michael Strogoff himself (MS-033-018).
- [[Ivan Ogareff]] — the Russian renegade who serves as his lieutenant and prompts the sentence; the Emir's headsman "carried out the sentences of Feofar-Khan against offenders" (MS-024-010).
- [[An Antarctic Mystery]] — the novel whose southern quest he guides under the name Hunt.

description: 'The carpenter of the brig Forward in The Adventures of Captain Hatteras, a steadfast craftsman and one of the four men chosen for Hatteras''s overland dash toward the Pole.'
